# BCS — boundaries around systems, not objects

> The Branded Component System: the successor to Entity-Component-System (ECS) adapted to the BEAM, where the only
> values that cross a system boundary are **branded identities and messages about them**. The architecture the whole
> echo stack realizes — and the figure source the /redis-patterns and /echomq courses draw from. Served at /bcs.

## When & why to read this

Read this for the **architecture**, not a single technology: why a 14-character branded snowflake is the *type*
checked at every boundary; why a component is data with no behaviour; why a system is a process that owns its private
state and shares nothing. Classic ECS packs components into shared contiguous arrays for cache locality; BCS gives
that up for **distribution + fault-tolerance** on the BEAM — a system is a process, the entity is a branded id, and
no object graph or embedded id list ever crosses a boundary. This course is the **source of truth** (the manuscript
`bcs.N.md`); the consumer courses door INTO it. The visual identity is the "contract-sheet" (warm paper, mono-forward,
numbered sections, frozen-transcript evidence). The worked system is **codemojex**; the engine is **Valkey 9**; the
durable floor is its own course, /echo-persistence.

## Navigation — the chapters

### B0 · Overview — /bcs/overview
The one relocation; identity as the contract; the stack and the floor.
- identity-as-contract · stack-and-floor · the-relocation

### B1 · The ideas — /bcs/ideas
The system substrate; the identity contract read as architecture; ECS → BCS.
- ecs-to-bcs · the-contract · the-substrate

### B2 · The Elixir core — /bcs/elixir-core
The law on OTP: the branded-id codec, property stores, archetypes (compose by fold), relations as systems, the CHAMP forest, the gate.
- otp-application · property-stores · archetypes · relations · the-champ-database · gates

### B3 · The Bus — /bcs/bus
EchoMQ, the Valkey-native bus where the BCS law meets the network: the keyspace as the contract on the wire, jobs + fair lanes, the Stream Tier.
- the-keyspace · jobs-and-lanes · the-stream-tier

### B4 · The Store — /bcs/store
EchoStore, the declared near-cache: one-fill-per-herd, coherence by mint time (newer-wins, the version IS the branded id).
- the-near-cache · one-fill-per-herd · coherence

### The Cache (in depth) — /bcs/cache
The near-cache built out: cache-aside, the single-writer ring, the lane that remembers, coherence by mint time.
- cache-aside · single-writer-ring · the-lane-that-remembers · coherence-by-mint-time

### B5 · Persistence — /bcs/persistence
The durable floor: the single-writer engine, the lazy reader, the portable remote (the /echo-persistence narrative).
- the-engine · the-lazy-reader · the-remote

### B6 · Together — /bcs/together
The four libraries as one umbrella; one write down, one read up.
- the-umbrella · the-write-path · the-read-path

### B7 · codemojex — /bcs/codemojex
The worked consumer: branded systems, rooms + modes, fair-lane guesses, scoring + settlement, the economy, the live surface.
- branded-systems · rooms-and-modes · guesses-on-fair-lanes · scoring-and-settlement · the-economy · the-live-surface

### B8 · On Fly — /bcs/fly
Production on Fly: the release image, Valkey on a Fly machine, EchoMQ setup, the fly.toml + the local stack.
- the-release-and-the-image · valkey-on-a-fly-machine · echomq-setup-and-monitoring · the-fly-config-and-the-local-stack

## The markdown source (the manuscript — the figure source)

- **The manuscript chapters** (B0–B8, the authoritative figure source quoted verbatim by all three courses): `docs/echo/bcs/bcs.0.md` … `docs/echo/bcs/bcs.8.md`
- **The preface** (the added persistence-floor movement) + the TOC: `docs/echo/bcs/bcs.preface.md`, `docs/echo/bcs/bcs.toc.md`
- **The branded-id contract & the cross-course calibration guide**: `docs/echo/bcs/bcs.course-tooling.guide.md`

- [/echomq](/echomq) — EchoMQ, the bus (B3) in depth
- [/echo-persistence](/echo-persistence) — the durable floor (B5) as its own course
